title: inspect: preserve secret target name in env masking
body:
Fixes #28075

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

For more detailed information, please review our contributing guidelines:
https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests
-->

#### Checklist

Ensure you have completed the following checklist for your pull request to be reviewed:
<!-- Use [x] to mark as done, or click the checkbox after opening PR -->

- [x] Certify you wrote the patch or otherwise have the right to pass it on as an open-source patch by signing all
commits. (`git commit -s`). (If needed, use `git commit -s --amend`).  The author email must match
the sign-off email address. See [CONTRIBUTING.md](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#sign-your-prs)
for more information.
- [x] Referenced issues using `Fixes: #28075 ` in commit message (if applicable)
- [x] [Tests](https://github.com/containers/podman/tree/main/test#readme) have been added/updated (or no tests are needed)
- [ ] [Documentation](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/docs/README.md) has been updated (or no documentation changes are needed)
- [x] All commits pass `make validatepr` (format/lint checks)
- [ ] [Release note](https://github.com/kubernetes/community/blob/master/contributors/guide/release-notes.md) entered in the section below (or `None` if no user-facing changes)

When a container uses a secret with a custom target (e.g., --secret name=foo,target=BAR), podman inspect incorrectly masks the environment variable using the secret name (foo=*****) instead of the target name (BAR=*****). Fix: This patch updates generateInspectContainerConfig to use the environment variable key (envName) when constructing the masked string, ensuring the output matches the container's actual environment. Verification: Verified locally on Ubuntu 24.04. podman run ... --secret test,target=MY_VAR now correctly displays MY_VAR=***** in podman inspect.

comments.body:
@jankaluza The CI is failing on tests-included because I haven't added a regression test.

Since this is a trivial display-only fix (+2/-2 lines) that only affects podman inspect output and not the runtime behavior, could you please apply the No New Tests label? Or should I add a specific E2E test case for this?
thanks for the PR, @amyssnippet . Please, try to address the validate comments first. you can run the check locally with `make validatepr`

@inknos can u add No New Tests label? as i think no tests are needed here

edit: its a  display-only fix

> thanks for the PR, @amyssnippet . Please, try to address the validate comments first. you can run the check locally with `make validatepr`

this ran successfully if i remove the noise from documentation generator trying to format text layout and are flaky for my changes
[NON-BLOCKING] Packit jobs failed. @containers/packit-build please check. Everyone else, feel free to ignore.
LGTM, but would like a test to prevent regression on this .

> can u add No New Tests label? as i think no tests are needed here

@amyssnippet  I also feel a regression test is needed. do you need any pointers to that?
@inknos and @baude , i need to address some more updates as ongoing discussion with the author of the issue, will solve it soon and add the tests too

@mheon @inknos @baude @danishprakash now the issue is successfully solved

edit: i added test too

@amyssnippet thanks for the PR!

Changes LGTM, a couple of falining tests that I restarted, I'm not sure if they are flakes or not.

@TomSweeneyRedHat , thanks for your review, and also right now there is only one failing ci test, and i got deep and saw the logs, it is failing during the preparation phase of the script. so guide me what should i do further??

```
No matching repositories for testing-farm-tag-repository. 
Add "--help" for more information about the arguments.
```

the script `dnf-repo-setup.sh` tried to run `dnf upgrade` with `--disable-repo=testing-farm-tag-repository` but i think the repo doesnt exists on the guest ci server. and as the script uses `set -eo pipefail`, the moment dnf complained about the missing repository, the whole script crashed with exit code 2.

I think that failed test is issue on testing-farm side. It's not caused by the code change introduced in this PR.
LGTM. Just the `Infof` logging with `DEBUG:` prefix seems weird to me.
Testing farm tests are advisory only, failure is allowed.
